overview: This U/B Legacy tempo deck leverages cheap spells and life-dropping effects to rapidly scale Death's Shadow into a dominant threat. Moonshadow provides early board presence and consistent life reduction, while Thoughtseize disrupts opponent strategies to protect the game plan. The deck aims to stabilize with a massive Shadow before opponents can recover or stabilize.
win_conditions: Scaled Death's Shadow overwhelming opponent's board
Card advantage leading to lethal Shadow attacks

keep_criteria: Hands with Thoughtseize and cheap spells
Hands containing Moonshadow for early life drop
Hands with multiple low-cost cards to pump Shadow
Keep hands with 2–3 lands, at least one Thoughtseize, and two sources of life loss (e.g., Dark Confidant, Moonshadow, Blood Crypt, or similar).
Keep hands with Death’s Shadow + 2+ life loss outlets + discard.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking cheap spells or life-dropping effects
Hands without Thoughtseize or early interaction
Mulligan hands with 3+ lands, no life loss outlets, or only one land with no early interaction.

early_game: Cast Moonshadow to drop life and establish board presence
Use Thoughtseize to remove key opponent threats or answers
Play cheap spells to gradually reduce life total
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Thoughtseize (target removal, ramp, or early threat).
Turn 2: Play a land, cast Dark Confidant or Moonshadow to establish life loss.
If Death’s Shadow is in hand and you have 2+ life loss sources, cast it on turn 2 or 3.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast key spells at instant speed when needed.
mid_game: Cast Death's Shadow and immediately pump it with life loss
Sequence Moonshadow's ability before combat to maximize power
Maintain card advantage while blocking or attacking with Shadow
Prioritize life loss outlets over card draw once Death’s Shadow is on board.
Use Thoughtseize to remove opponent’s interaction rather than redundant threats.
Manage your life total carefully—never drop below 1 unless you can immediately stabilize or win.
Commit Death’s Shadow only when you have consistent life loss (2+ sources) and opponent has no immediate sweepers or targeted removal in hand.
Hold back if they show signs of a board wipe or heavy interaction.
Use discard to disrupt their development rather than trading creatures.
late_game: Use massive Death's Shadow to clear board or close game
Avoid dropping to 0 life while maintaining pressure
Leverage card draw and removal to outlast opponent
Death’s Shadow (often 13+ power), Moonshadow, or a late-game Dark Confidant.
Close the game by attacking with Shadow while using discard to remove blockers or protection, or by out-valuing them with card advantage.
Go all-in when you have 2+ life loss sources, opponent has no sweepers or removal in hand, and you can reasonably close the game in 1–2 turns.
Also commit when you can discard their only answer to Shadow.

key_cards.name: Death's Shadow
key_cards.role: Primary win condition and scalable threat
key_cards.notes: Cast when life is low enough to enter at high power; avoid dropping to 0 life or it dies

key_cards.name: Moonshadow
key_cards.notes: Use its ability to reduce life before casting Shadow; provides evasion and early board presence

key_cards.name: Thoughtseize
key_cards.notes: Hold up for key threats or answers; use to find life-dropping spells or protect Shadow

sequencing_tips: Always activate Moonshadow's ability before casting Death's Shadow to ensure it enters at maximum power
Hold Thoughtseize for opponent's key threats or removal spells
Drop life gradually using cheap spells to avoid accidentally hitting 0
Sequence removal after opponent commits resources to maximize card advantage
Common mistakes include casting Shadow too early without enough life loss, overcommitting to Dark Confidant without protection, and mismanaging your life total.
Moonshadow can serve as both a life loss outlet and a protective tool—use it flexibly.
Always calculate your life total before combat to avoid accidental loss.
Format legality of specific cards may vary, so adjust land and spell counts accordingly.

matchups.advice: Pump Death's Shadow quickly to block early threats and use Thoughtseize on key finishers
Disrupt early game plans with Thoughtseize and use Shadow as a resilient threat that survives interaction
Use Thoughtseize to disrupt key combo pieces and pressure life total with a scaled Shadow
Use Thoughtseize to remove counterspells. Cast Shadow at end step or when they’re tapped out. Leverage Moonshadow’s abilities to protect your plays or value out their interaction.
Thoughtseize for mana dorks, ramp spells, or extra lands. Apply early pressure to force them to interact before they stabilize. Use Surgical Strike to remove key threats.

Win the discard war by prioritizing life loss outlets and Shadow. Surgical Strike their Shadow or key interaction. Go Blank to disrupt their life loss engine. Track life totals carefully to out-value them in combat.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers, targeted removal, counterspells, and graveyard hate.
Note when opponents tap out for threats versus holding up interaction, and track their life total to calculate Death’s Shadow’s power accurately.
